{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "SME Loan Officer",
  "description": "Spectrum Microfinance Bank is a customer-centric retail financial institution driven by technology; licensed by the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) to support economic growth and development through microfinancing. Spectrum Microfinance Bank offers savings, loan, and investment opportunities to a wide range of customers with the aim of creating self-sufficient individuals and sustainable businesses.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;SME Loan Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos

About the Role


	We are a reputable Microfinance Bank, seeking an experienced and result-driven SME Loan Officer to drive business growth through the acquisition and management of small and medium enterprise (SME) clients.
	The ideal candidate will have strong credit analysis skills, business development capability, and the ability to manage a healthy loan portfolio.


Key Responsibilities


	Source and onboard SME clients through strategic business development initiatives.
	Build and maintain strong client relationships to drive retention and cross-selling opportunities.
	Assess client needs and structure appropriate loan solutions.
	Conduct financial analysis, site visits, and credit assessments of SME businesses.
	Prepare credit proposals and present for approval in line with internal policies.
	Monitor loan performance, ensure timely repayments, and manage delinquent accounts.
	Maintain accurate records and ensure compliance with KYC, AML, and regulatory requirements.


Performance Metrics (KPIs)


	Loan disbursement volume
	Number of new SME clients acquired
	Portfolio quality (PAR)
	Loan repayment and collections performance.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Finance, Business Administration, Economics, or related field.
	Minimum of 3&ndash;5 years of experience in SME lending, credit analysis, or commercial banking.
	Strong understanding of financial statements and credit risk assessment.
	Proven track record in client acquisition and loan portfolio management.
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and relationship management skills.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;careers@spectrummfb.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-06-08",

SME Loan Officer Job at Spectrum Microfinance Bank




        
    

        
        Spectrum Microfinance Bank is a customer-centric retail financial institution driven by technology; licensed by the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) to support economic growth and development through microfinancing. Spectrum Microfinance Bank offers savings, loan, and investment opportunities to a wide range of customers with the aim of creating self-sufficient individuals and sustainable businesses.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: SME Loan Officer

Job Location: Lagos

About the Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are a reputable Microfinance Bank, seeking an experienced and result-driven SME Loan Officer to drive business growth through the acquisition and management of small and medium enterprise (SME) clients.
	The ideal candidate will have strong credit analysis skills, business development capability, and the ability to manage a healthy loan portfolio.


Key Responsibilities


	Source and onboard SME clients through strategic business development initiatives.
	Build and maintain strong client relationships to drive retention and cross-selling opportunities.
	Assess client needs and structure appropriate loan solutions.
	Conduct financial analysis, site visits, and credit assessments of SME businesses.
	Prepare credit proposals and present for approval in line with internal policies.
	Monitor loan performance, ensure timely repayments, and manage delinquent accounts.
	Maintain accurate records and ensure compliance with KYC, AML, and regulatory requirements.


Performance Metrics (KPIs)


	Loan disbursement volume
	Number of new SME clients acquired
	Portfolio quality (PAR)
	Loan repayment and collections performance.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Business Administration, Economics, or related field.
	Minimum of 3–5 years of experience in SME lending, credit analysis, or commercial banking.
	Strong understanding of financial statements and credit risk assessment.
	Proven track record in client acquisition and loan portfolio management.
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and relationship management skills.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Logistics Manager",
  "description": "Solar Nigachem stands out as a reliable and trustworthy partner. Our unparalleled expertise, commitment to safety, cutting-edge technology, and customer-centric approach makes us a go-to provider for the following sectors, Mining, road construction, Defense projects, Quarries, Seismic Exploration, Tunneling, Hydro Projects and all others - our customized solutions and exceptional customer support ensure that our clients receive the highest level of assistance and satisfaction. With Solar Nigachem, mining operations can rely on their explosives to enhance productivity, efficiency, and safety, ultimately contributing to the success and sustainability of the mining industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Logistics Manager

Job&nbsp;&nbsp;Location:&nbsp;Abanla (Ibadan), Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Function


	Ensure accurate receipts, storage and issuance of explosive materials
	Planing secure, efficient routes for transport, and Coordinate timely dispatch of material to clients
	Conduct regular checks, documentation receives and audit.
	Monitor stock levels of raw materials, spare parts, and finished goods.
	Monitor vehicle utilization, fuel usage, and maintenance schedules
	Coordinate movement of materials between Magazines and to clients destinatiion.
	Maintain delivery notes, waybills, invoices, customs documents (if imports/exports are involved).
	Ensure compliance with company policies and government regulations.
	Schedule servicing, repairs, licensing, and insurance renewals.
	Eradictaelosses caused from damage, theft, or expired explosives
	Assist in managingMagazine staff, drivers, and store Officers.
	Train staff on procedures and safety protocols
	Ensuring and supervising strict adherence to safety and security protocols to prevent unauthorized access, including security requirements for transporting hazardous materials.
	Assist in Managing therelationships and compliance ofexplosive permit with therelevants government body.


Requirements


	A minimum of 5 - 10 years of experience in Logistics, Warehouse Operations, Supply chain, procurement or Transportation.
	B.sc or MBA / Msc in Logistics Management, Supply chain Management, Businedd Administration or related field.
	The ideal candidate should be a member of Association for Supply Chain Management (ASCM)
	A member of Chartered Institude of Logistics and Transport (CILT)Nigeria.
	Strong understanding of Inventory Control system.
	Software and systems knowledge: Microsoft excel and other ERP system.
	Knowledge of Nigeria laws regarding explosive handling, including obtaining permits from the Ministry of Mines and steel Development, End- User Certificates (EUCs) and complying with the NSA regulations.
	Excellent communication skill both written and verbal.
	Ability to plan and organise work process
	Strong leadership skill
	Ability to analyse logistics data, budget preparation and cost control experience.
	Willingness to travel between plants, magazinesand customer locations when necessary.
	Good knowledge of transportation laws, warehouse safety and regulatory compliance.
	Strong leadership and problem solving skill.


Salary
N350,000 - N400,000 monthly.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;hr@solarnigachem.com.ngusing the Job Position as subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-31",

Logistics Manager Job at Solar Nigachem Limited




        
    

        
        Solar Nigachem stands out as a reliable and trustworthy partner. Our unparalleled expertise, commitment to safety, cutting-edge technology, and customer-centric approach makes us a go-to provider for the following sectors, Mining, road construction, Defense projects, Quarries, Seismic Exploration, Tunneling, Hydro Projects and all others - our customized solutions and exceptional customer support ensure that our clients receive the highest level of assistance and satisfaction. With Solar Nigachem, mining operations can rely on their explosives to enhance productivity, efficiency, and safety, ultimately contributing to the success and sustainability of the mining industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Position: Logistics Manager

Job  Location: Abanla (Ibadan), Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Function


	Ensure accurate receipts, storage and issuance of explosive materials
	Planing secure, efficient routes for transport, and Coordinate timely dispatch of material to clients
	Conduct regular checks, documentation receives and audit.
	Monitor stock levels of raw materials, spare parts, and finished goods.
	Monitor vehicle utilization, fuel usage, and maintenance schedules
	Coordinate movement of materials between Magazines and to clients destinatiion.
	Maintain delivery notes, waybills, invoices, customs documents (if imports/exports are involved).
	Ensure compliance with company policies and government regulations.
	Schedule servicing, repairs, licensing, and insurance renewals.
	Eradictaelosses caused from damage, theft, or expired explosives
	Assist in managingMagazine staff, drivers, and store Officers.
	Train staff on procedures and safety protocols
	Ensuring and supervising strict adherence to safety and security protocols to prevent unauthorized access, including security requirements for transporting hazardous materials.
	Assist in Managing therelationships and compliance ofexplosive permit with therelevants government body.


Requirements


	A minimum of 5 - 10 years of experience in Logistics, Warehouse Operations, Supply chain, procurement or Transportation.
	B.sc or MBA / Msc in Logistics Management, Supply chain Management, Businedd Administration or related field.
	The ideal candidate should be a member of Association for Supply Chain Management (ASCM)
	A member of Chartered Institude of Logistics and Transport (CILT)Nigeria.
	Strong understanding of Inventory Control system.
	Software and systems knowledge: Microsoft excel and other ERP system.
	Knowledge of Nigeria laws regarding explosive handling, including obtaining permits from the Ministry of Mines and steel Development, End- User Certificates (EUCs) and complying with the NSA regulations.
	Excellent communication skill both written and verbal.
	Ability to plan and organise work process
	Strong leadership skill
	Ability to analyse logistics data, budget preparation and cost control experience.
	Willingness to travel between plants, magazinesand customer locations when necessary.
	Good knowledge of transportation laws, warehouse safety and regulatory compliance.
	Strong leadership and problem solving skill.


Salary
N350,000 - N400,000 monthly.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected]using the Job Position as subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Vacancies at Quanlee Overseas Warehouse Nigeria Limited
content:
Quanlee Overseas Warehouse Nigeria Limited has high-quality supply chain resources in China and provides the best quality auto parts for overseas vehicle parts distributors and demanders. The company has established a systematic quality management guarantee system, a comprehensive supply chain and a professional technical team.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Sales Representative

Job Locations: Oregun - Lagos, Ekiti, Ogun, Ondo, Osun and Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for proactive and results-driven Sales Representatives to help expand our customer network and grow sales across assigned regions.
	The ideal candidate should be confident communicating with customers, building relationships, identifying business opportunities, and managing client accounts professionally.
	This role involves field sales, customer relationship management, market expansion, and supporting the company’s sales and distribution operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify and develop new customers within assigned territories
	Visit customers regularly and maintain strong relationships
	Promote and sell company products to customers and distributors
	Meet monthly and quarterly sales targets
	Create and manage customer records accurately
	Follow up on customer payments and outstanding invoices
	Assist customers with orders, product inquiries, and after-sales support
	Prepare daily and weekly sales activity reports
	Monitor market trends, competitor activities, and customer needs
	Coordinate with warehouse and operations teams for order fulfillment and delivery
	Represent the company professionally during customer interactions and negotiations
	Support the company’s growth strategy within assigned regions


Requirements


	Minimum of Higher National Diploma (HND), OND, or Bachelor’s Degree
	Previous sales experience is an advantage
	Strong communication and negotiation skills
	Ability to work independently and meet targets
	Good interpersonal and relationship management skills
	Must be proactive, honest, and responsible
	Ability to use smartphones, WhatsApp, and basic digital tools for communication and reporting
	Willingness to travel within assigned locations
	Ability to speak English fluently


Preferred Qualities:


	Strong work ethic
	Customer-focused mindset
	Team player
	Problem-solving ability
	Ability to work under pressure
	Eagerness to learn and grow within the company.


Application Closing Date: 14th May, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:  [email protected] using "Name - Marketer", e.g.: "Adebayo- Marketer" as the subject of the mail.

 

2.) Marketer

Job Location: Enugu, Nigeria | Covering South East & South Region
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities 


	Develop marketing strategies to grow our client base in the South East & South
	Drive sales growth for the company
	Build strong relationships with auto parts distributors & corporate clients
	Promote our services online and offline
	Research the market & monitor competitors
	Travel for business when required.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Marketing, Business, Engineering, Auto-related or similar fields
	2+ years of experience in marketing, sales or logistics
	Fluent in English | Knowledge of Chinese is a plus
	Experience in auto parts/auto industry is an advantage
	Age: 25–35 | Male or Female.


Salary


	N80,000 monthly + performance commission
	Final pay depends on experience & results.


Application Closing Date: 20th May, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Marketer – Enugu" as the subject of the mail.

Note


	For more enquiries, call / Whatsapp: 08064076643
	Candidates with auto parts experience will be given priority. Business trips are required.


 

 

3.) Cook / House Help

Job Location: Kano
Employment Type: Full-time

Requirements


	Cook / house help for Chinese employer
	Should know how to carry out basic house keeping chores in the house.
	Must be a female.
	Laundry services using washing machine might be required.


Salary
N100,000 per month.

Application Closing Date: 8th June, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Posas the subject of the mail.

     

    

        Click here to Apply

Recovery Officer Job at FLOCITI Gold Resources Ltd




        
    

        
        FLOCITI Gold Resources Ltd was incorporated as a licensed limited liability company in 2021 to provide financial solutions to Entrepreneurs, SMEs & Business owners. The company started informally in June 2021 but formally commenced business operations in January 2022. The Company has since witnessed steady growth to become a valuable Company to watch out for in the finance space in West Africa; creating wealth and providing solutions to barriers in financing for Small & Medium Scale Enterprises/Business Owners.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Recovery Officer

Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Follow up on overdue loans and outstanding payments.
	Engage clients professionally to ensure prompt repayment.
	Negotiate repayment plans where necessary.
	Prepare recovery reports and updates.
	Ensure compliance with company recovery procedures and policies.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND / HND / B.Sc in any discipline.
	Good communication and negotiation skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet targets.
	Previous experience in loan recovery or collections is an added advantage.


Salary
N100,000 - N150,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidate should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Hotel Purchasing Officer",
  "description": "Yimei Nigeria Limited is a hospitality and hotel management company operating as part of the broader Yimei Group, an international hotel chain focused on the fast-growing African market. The company specializes in hotel development, management, and franchising, offering a range of accommodation options from budget to upscale through its various hotel brands.

Established in Nigeria in 2020, Yimei Nigeria Limited is headquartered in Ikeja, Lagos, and has rapidly expanded its footprint with multiple hotel operations across Lagos. Yimei focuses on delivering quality hospitality services by combining modern hotel management systems, customer-centric service, and strategic business expansion. The company also supports hotel owners and investors through professional management solutions, operational systems, and market-driven strategies.&nbsp;Driven by a vision to become a leading hotel operator in Africa, Yimei Nigeria Limited continues to grow its presence by developing new properties and expanding partnerships within the hospitality industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Hotel Purchasing Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Procure daily operational supplies (F&amp;B ingredients, linens, guest amenities, office supplies, engineering parts).
	Source, evaluate, and manage suppliers; conduct regular RFQ, price comparison, and negotiation to control costs.
	Create purchasing plans based on requisitions; ensure timely, quality, and accurate delivery.
	Monitor inventory levels to avoid overstock or stockouts; coordinate with Finance for payment.
	Strictly follow hotel purchasing policies and integrity clauses.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidate should send their CV&nbsp;to:&nbsp;oluwaseyi.agunbiade@yiwill.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Hotel Purchasing Officer Job at Yimei Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Yimei Nigeria Limited is a hospitality and hotel management company operating as part of the broader Yimei Group, an international hotel chain focused on the fast-growing African market. The company specializes in hotel development, management, and franchising, offering a range of accommodation options from budget to upscale through its various hotel brands.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Established in Nigeria in 2020, Yimei Nigeria Limited is headquartered in Ikeja, Lagos, and has rapidly expanded its footprint with multiple hotel operations across Lagos. Yimei focuses on delivering quality hospitality services by combining modern hotel management systems, customer-centric service, and strategic business expansion. The company also supports hotel owners and investors through professional management solutions, operational systems, and market-driven strategies. Driven by a vision to become a leading hotel operator in Africa, Yimei Nigeria Limited continues to grow its presence by developing new properties and expanding partnerships within the hospitality industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Hotel Purchasing Officer

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Procure daily operational supplies (F&B ingredients, linens, guest amenities, office supplies, engineering parts).
	Source, evaluate, and manage suppliers; conduct regular RFQ, price comparison, and negotiation to control costs.
	Create purchasing plans based on requisitions; ensure timely, quality, and accurate delivery.
	Monitor inventory levels to avoid overstock or stockouts; coordinate with Finance for payment.
	Strictly follow hotel purchasing policies and integrity clauses.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidate should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
